prompt: Storage billing for our artifact registry is coming out absurdly low and I've stopped trusting the whole ledger. The numbers only go wrong for heavy tenants: ml-platform keeps about 15 GiB of model checkpoints in three objects and the month-end rollup bills them for about 3 GiB; a tenant whose only object is a 4 GiB firmware image shows up as using nothing at all; the quota gate happily waves uploads through for tenants that are plainly gigabytes over their limit; and the 'largest tenants' report ranks a tenant holding one big disk image below tenants holding a fraction of that in small files. Meanwhile every small tenant reconciles to the byte — dev and staging (where nothing tops a few hundred MiB) have never disagreed with a manual count, which is exactly why this shipped. The charging rules themselves are unchanged and correct: uploads are charged in whole 4 KiB blocks, re-uploading a key replaces the old charge, deletes free it, landing exactly on quota is allowed. `cargo test --offline` reproduces everything I've described. Fix src/lib.rs — don't touch tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore.
